techniques.evidence_quote: the attacker abused stolen OAuth user tokens issued to two third-party OAuth integrators, Heroku and Travis CI
an attacker abused stolen OAuth user tokens issued to two third-party OAuth integrators, Heroku and Travis CI
the actor was able to exfiltrate a set of private npm repositories, some of which included secrets such as AWS access keys
private npm repositories, some of which included secrets such as AWS access keys
Using one of these AWS access keys, the actor was able to gain access to npm's AWS infrastructure
the actor was able to exfiltrate an older backup of the skimdb.npmjs.com mirror
the actor exfiltrated a set of npm inventory CSV files containing directory listings of the S3 buckets

attack_chain.description: Attacker obtained OAuth user tokens issued to third-party GitHub integrators Heroku and Travis CI (not via compromise of GitHub itself).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker abused stolen OAuth user tokens issued to two third-party OAuth integrators, Heroku and Travis CI

attack_chain.description: Attacker used the stolen third-party OAuth tokens to authenticate to GitHub.com and access npm's private repositories via the trusted integrator relationship.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: to download data from dozens of GitHub.com organizations. One of the victim organizations impacted was npm.

evidence_chunks.quote: to download data from dozens of GitHub.com organizations. One of the victim organizations impacted was npm.
to download data from dozens of GitHub.com organizations. One of the victim organizations impacted was npm.

attack_chain.description: Attacker exfiltrated a set of private npm GitHub repositories containing source code and secrets.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the actor was able to exfiltrate a set of private npm repositories

evidence_chunks.quote: the actor was able to exfiltrate a set of private npm repositories
the actor was able to exfiltrate a set of private npm repositories

attack_chain.description: Attacker harvested AWS access keys stored as secrets inside the stolen private npm repositories.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: some of which included secrets such as AWS access keys

evidence_chunks.quote: some of which included secrets such as AWS access keys
some of which included secrets such as AWS access keys

attack_chain.description: Attacker used the stolen AWS access keys to authenticate to and access npm's AWS infrastructure.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Using one of these AWS access keys, the actor was able to gain access to npm's AWS infrastructure

attack_chain.description: Attacker enumerated S3 buckets and inventory, obtaining CSV directory listings of private package names and versions.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a set of npm inventory CSV files containing directory listings of the S3 buckets storing packages for the npm registry

evidence_chunks.quote: a set of npm inventory CSV files containing directory listings of the S3 buckets storing packages for the npm registry
a set of npm inventory CSV files containing directory listings of the S3 buckets storing packages for the npm registry

attack_chain.description: Attacker exfiltrated an older backup of skimdb.npmjs.com (April 7, 2021) from npm's AWS cloud storage, containing package metadata/manifests and a 2015 user archive with ~100k usernames, password hashes, and emails.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the actor was able to exfiltrate an older backup of the skimdb.npmjs.com mirror

evidence_chunks.quote: the actor was able to exfiltrate an older backup of the skimdb.npmjs.com mirror
the actor was able to exfiltrate an older backup of the skimdb.npmjs.com mirror

attack_chain.description: Attacker exfiltrated private package contents from S3 belonging to two specific npm customer organizations.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the actor exfiltrated a small subset of private package contents belonging to two specific organizations

evidence_chunks.quote: the actor exfiltrated a small subset of private package contents belonging to two specific organizations
the actor exfiltrated a small subset of private package contents belonging to two specific organizations

initial_access_vector: Stolen OAuth user tokens issued to third-party GitHub integrators (Heroku and Travis CI) used to access npm's private GitHub repositories.
persistence_mechanism: None documented; access was maintained via stolen OAuth tokens and AWS access keys for the duration of the intrusion.
privilege_escalation: Pivoted from GitHub repo access to AWS infrastructure by harvesting AWS access keys hardcoded in private npm repositories.
